qa_pairs.question: How do you include an interpreted User-Defined Function (UDF) in an Ansys Fluent simulation using a journal file?
qa_pairs.answer: To include an interpreted UDF in an Ansys Fluent simulation via a journal file, add the command `define/user-defined/interpreted-functions "sampleudf.c" "cpp" 10000 no` into your journal file. This command should be placed before any commands that read in or initialize the case/data files, and `sampleudf.c` should be replaced with the actual name of your UDF source file.

qa_pairs.question: What considerations are necessary for an interpreted User-Defined Function (UDF) to work correctly and be properly controlled by an Ansys Fluent journal file, especially when running parallel jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: To ensure an interpreted UDF works correctly and is controlled by a Fluent journal file: the UDF should be placed in the same directory as your journal, case, and data files. Additionally, before uploading your case file, open it in the Fluent GUI, remove any managed UDF definitions, and then resave it, so only the journal file commands are in control. For parallel Ansys Fluent jobs, the interpreted UDF will also need to be parallelized.
